rules.title: Suspicious server URL
rules.description: IPs, ngrok/tunnel hosts, free-TLD or .onion in server URL
rules.remediation: Use only verified, stable hostnames for MCP server URLs. Avoid IPs, free TLDs, and tunnel services.

rules.title: Unencrypted remote server
rules.description: Remote MCP server using HTTP instead of HTTPS
rules.remediation: Switch to HTTPS. Never use HTTP for remote MCP servers.

rules.title: Hardcoded credential in env
rules.description: API key / token / private-key material in server env config
rules.remediation: Move credentials to environment variables or a secrets manager. Never hardcode tokens in MCP config files.

rules.title: Credential in command args
rules.description: Token passed via CLI args — visible in process listings
rules.remediation: Use environment variables instead of command-line arguments for credentials.

rules.title: Credential pattern in file
rules.description: Token pattern detected in raw config file outside structured fields
rules.remediation: Remove credentials from config files. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.title: Prompt injection in tool desc
rules.description: Injection patterns in tool descriptions hijack agent behavior
rules.remediation: Audit tool descriptions for injected instructions. Only use MCP servers from trusted sources.

rules.title: Dangerous permission granted
rules.description: Server requests a high-risk capability (shell_exec, admin, sudo, ...)
rules.remediation: Apply least-privilege. Only grant permissions explicitly required by the server.

rules.title: Unverified package server
rules.description: Unpinned npx/uvx/pip command — supply-chain attack surface
rules.remediation: Pin exact package versions (e.g., npx package@1.2.3). Verify checksums. Review package source before use.

rules.title: TLS certificate expiring soon
rules.description: Remote MCP server's TLS certificate expires within 14 days
rules.remediation: Renew the TLS certificate before it expires. Use Let's Encrypt + auto-renewal so this doesn't recur.

rules.title: TLS certificate expired
rules.description: Remote MCP server's TLS certificate is expired or fails verification
rules.remediation: Renew the TLS certificate immediately. Configure auto-renewal (Let's Encrypt + certbot / caddy).

rules.title: Remote endpoint serves HTTP
rules.description: Remote MCP endpoint uses HTTP — all traffic, including credentials, is observable on the network path
rules.remediation: Migrate the endpoint to HTTPS. Never run remote MCP over HTTP, even on 'internal' networks.

rules.title: Remote endpoint unreachable or failing
rules.description: Remote MCP server returns a non-success HTTP status or is unreachable
rules.remediation: Check server logs, DNS, firewall, and TLS configuration. A consistently failing MCP endpoint is unusable.

rules.title: Response header discloses software identity
rules.description: Response headers (Server, X-Powered-By, etc.) reveal software versions that make CVE-targeted attacks easier
rules.remediation: Configure the server to omit or sanitize disclosure headers. nginx: server_tokens off; — apache: ServerTokens Prod + ServerSignature Off — express.js: app.disable('x-powered-by').
